zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Lamb de Prez於1281年5月12日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯特盧德時，她的父親Karl de Prez21歲，母親Mary Eliza Lamb21歲。她於1300年與Ralph de Harding在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1304年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其享年24歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Lamb de Prez于1281年5月12日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯特盧德时，她的父亲Karl de Prez21岁，母亲Mary Eliza Lamb21岁。她于1300年与Ralph de Harding在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1304年在英国英格兰坎布利其爾坎布利其享年24岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Prez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .
Breton (Pérez): variant of Pérès (see Peres ).

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
Czech (Pařez): nickname from pařez ‘(tree) stump’.
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

nameMeanings.name: Lamb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

variants.name: Lambert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

variants.name: Lamison
meanings: English: patronymic from   Lamb   2.

variants.name: Lamm
meanings: Irish: Cam, crooked, bent, curved

variants.name: Lamkin
meanings: English: from a pet form of   Lamb   1.

variants.name: Lambson
meanings: English: patronymic from   Lamb   1.

variants.name: Lambe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ciaran, Ethna.
Irish variant of English   Lamb  .

variants.name: Lambkin
meanings: English (Kent): from the Middle English and Flemish personal name Lambkin, a pet form of the personal name Lamb or Lambert .

variants.name: Lampen
meanings: English: from a pet form of   Lamb   1 and 2.

variants.name: Lampkin
meanings: English: from a pet form of   Lamb   1 and 2.

variants.name: Lampson
meanings: The Greek form of the Scripture name, which Simonis translates, 'sol eximius,' i.e. illustrious sun; the Rev. A. Jones 'splendid sun,' i.e. great joy and felicity; Tregelles, ' solar, like the sun; ' and Fuerstius, ' sonnenahnlicher ' that is, one who resembles the sun (from shemesh, the sun).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Lamb de Prez was born on 12 May 1281, in Stroud, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Karl de Prez, was 21 and her mother, Mary Eliza Lamb, was 21. She married Ralph de Harding in 1300, in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1304, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 24.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Lamb de Prez est née le 12 mai 1281, à Stroud, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Karl de Prez, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mary Eliza Lamb, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph de Harding en 1300, à Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1304, à Canteburge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 24 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Lamb de Prez am 12. Mai 1281 in Stroud, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Karl de Prez 21 und ihre Mutter Mary Eliza Lamb 21. Sie hat 1300 in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Ralph de Harding geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1304 im Alter von 24 Jahren in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Lamb de Prez è nata il il 12 maggio 1281 nel Stroud, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, Karl de Prez, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Mary Eliza Lamb, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Ralph de Harding nel 1300, nel Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1304, nel Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 24 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Lamb de Prezが1281年5月12日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアストロウドで生まれた時，父Karl de Prezは21歳，母Mary Eliza Lambは21歳であった。1300年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアカンブリッジでRalph de Hardingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1304年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジで24歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Lamb de Prez(이)가 1281년 5월 12일 영국 그루스스터실르 스트루드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Karl de Prez(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Eliza Lamb(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 캄브리지에서 Ralph de Harding(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1304년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 24세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Lamb de Prez nasceu em 12 de maio de 1281, em Stroud, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Karl de Prez, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mary Eliza Lamb, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Ralph de Harding em 1300, em Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1304, em Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 24 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Lamb de Prez родилась 12 мая 1281 г. в местности строуд, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Karl de Prez было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Eliza Lamb, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph de Harding в 1300 г. в местности камбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1304 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 24 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Lamb de Prez nació el 12 de mayo de 1281, en Stroud, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Karl de Prez, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mary Eliza Lamb, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Ralph de Harding en 1300, en Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1304, en Cantebriche, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 24 años.
